name: Muslim Advocates
mission: TO PROMOTE EQUALITY, LIBERTY AND JUSTICE FOR ALL, BY PROVIDING LEADERSHIP THROUGH LEGAL ADVOCACY, POLICY ENGAGEMENT AND CIVIC EDUCATION AND BY SERVING A...
theoryOfChange: Muslim Advocates outlines its strategic approach and how it measures impact in its Annual Report. The organization states, 'We measure our impact against three pillars: building community power, dismantling discriminatory systems, and demanding shared wellbeing.' These foundational principles guide their work through high-impact litigation, policy engagement, public advocacy, narrative-shifting communications, and training for advocates. They are committed to supporting those most impacted by structural racism and inequity, measuring success by communities' ability to develop, organize, and act together to shift narratives, influence decision-makers, and create transformative change. Their approach also involves leveraging litigation, coalitional advocacy, and legislative engagement to hold policymakers accountable, and using strategic communications to shift oppressive narratives about Muslim identity.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="5">7.8 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer against revenue fluctuations.
The charity has successfully secured <cite id="8">over $90,000 in legal awards and settlements</cite> through its litigation efforts.
It is one of the few legal advocacy groups <cite id="9">verified to accept zakat</cite> for civil rights protection.

strengths_deep_dive: Muslim Advocates demonstrates high organizational resilience through its <cite id="5">7.8 months of operating reserves</cite>, which is essential for a group involved in long-term <cite id="1">legal representation and litigation</cite>. Their governance structure is supported by a <cite id="11">10-member board with 100% independence</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of their <cite id="1">policy advocacy and civic education</cite> programs. Furthermore, the organization provides specialized <cite id="1">Know Your Rights materials</cite> and runs a <cite id="1">Muslim Charities Accreditation Program</cite> to strengthen the broader non-profit sector.
rich_narrative.summary: Muslim Advocates is a national civil rights organization that works to protect the liberties of American Muslims through <cite id="1">legal representation, litigation, and policy advocacy</cite>. The organization focuses on systemic change by <cite id="2">challenging discriminatory policies</cite> and providing civic education to empower communities. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $992,892</cite>, a significant decrease from previous years as reflected in a <cite id="4">three-year revenue CAGR of -62.5%</cite>. Despite this contraction, the charity maintains a <cite id="5">resilient liquidity position with 7.8 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring it can sustain operations during periods of financial volatility. The charity operates with a <cite id="6">program expense ratio of 54.9%</cite>, which is notably lower than the <cite id="7">78.7% median for advocacy peers</cite>. This indicates that a large portion of the budget is currently absorbed by administrative and fundraising needs. However, the organization remains a critical player in the legal space, having secured <cite id="8">$90,628 in legal awards and settlements</cite> to support its mission. For Muslim donors, the organization is <cite id="9">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving categories such as <cite id="10">fisabilillah (in the way of Allah) and amil (zakat administrators)</cite>.
